edits.slides.0.description: No brief, no client, no user research — so I invented my own constraints: make Wikipedia's techno content feel like the music sounds. A self-initiated scroll-telling site, built solo in Figma and Webflow.
edits.slides.1.label: The Brief
edits.slides.1.title: Wikipedia's layout didn't fit the story
edits.slides.1.content: Wikipedia covers techno accurately. None of it sounds like it feels. I set one constraint: take the same source material and make it feel like what it describes.

slides.0.headlineMetric.label: Live on Webflow
slides.0.headlineMetric.context: Self-initiated · Solo designer + developer · Figma + Webflow

slides.1.issues: Dense text walls with no visual hierarchy — information, not experience
Generic sidebar structure makes every topic feel identical
No pacing — every paragraph competes equally for attention
setFields.slides.4.title: Where information becomes atmosphere
setFields.slides.4.description: Monochrome grain, ASCII silhouettes, oversized type — every visual choice earned its place by what the content needed to feel.
setFields.slides.4.caption: The Techno Chronicles — live on Webflow

ops.reason: Second of three identical problem slides — retype to directions (Ideation) to surface the accepted/rejected design decisions and deliver the fastest senior-judgment signal in the deck.

slide.label: Ideation
slide.title: Directions I explored
slide.dir1Desc: Text-forward redesign — clean type over Wikipedia's layout. Rejected: it read as a design exercise, not a cultural experience.
slide.dir2Desc: Visual maximalism — photography, gradients, full-bleed texture. Rejected: decoration competed with the story instead of carrying it.
slide.dir3Desc: Rhythmic restraint — monochrome grain, ASCII portraits, scroll-triggered reveals. The visual language encodes the underground register without illustrating it. Works across every screen size.

ops.reason: Standalone responsive slide is redundant — the responsive constraint is covered in the accepted direction on the new Ideation slide. Three consecutive problem-type slides collapsed to one problem + one directions.
Outcomes slide misuses metric fields (Rhythm / Balance / Responsive / Clarity) as design properties, not results — retype to reflection, the required senior signal missing from this deck.

slide.label: Reflection
slide.title: What I'd do differently
whatWorked: The monochrome-grain system held the aesthetic together across every section — one committed rule, no style guide needed.
whatFailed: I shipped on gut feeling — no scroll-speed testing, no reader feedback before launch.
whatYoudDoDifferently: Test three scroll-timing variations with five readers before committing — pacing is the hardest thing to evaluate alone.
slide.whatYouLearned: Encoding attention through scroll timing is the same cognitive problem as controlling attention in a multi-step workflow. The constraint transfers.
slide.whatYouCouldntMeasure: Whether someone unfamiliar with techno still feels the genre's energy by the end — that was the whole point, and I never measured it.
